response.justification: The candidate is a strong individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling and testing-infrastructure credentials (test runners, CI/CD setup, Selenium training, MCP code generators), but the role is a people-leadership position and the resume shows no people-management experience at all, falling short of the 2-year minimum and the 3-year technical-leadership requirement. There is no evidence of roadmap ownership, performance management, or organizational change leadership, and the recent unexplained career gap adds uncertainty. He would be a plausible senior IC hire for this team, not its manager.
strengths: Hands-on developer-tools and testing-infrastructure background: authored test runners and assertion libraries, built CI/CD and device-orchestration tooling, created Selenium training
15+ years of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile (iOS/Apple TV), embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Clear mentoring and knowledge-sharing inclination through workshops, courses, refactoring dojos, and conference talks
concerns: No people-management experience, missing the explicit 2-year minimum for this Level II manager role
No formal technical-leadership role meeting the 3-year requirement; career trajectory is consistently individual-contributor
Unexplained career gap since October 2025, and mobile-platform exposure is dated and shallow relative to a large mobile ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
